mnemonics: RITA Runs In Threaded Animation (Runnable Interface, Thread class, Animation)
keyPoints: A single Runnable object can be used to create multiple threads that execute the same task concurrently.
Each thread is an independent instance of the Thread class, even if they share the same Runnable target.
The start() method must be called on each individual Thread object to begin execution, not on the Runnable object.
commonMistakes: Calling run() instead of start(), which executes the code in the current thread instead of creating a new one.
Assuming synchronized is automatically applied to the Runnable's run method; shared data accessed by multiple threads needs explicit synchronization.
Creating a new Thread with the same Runnable and expecting them to have separate instance variables; they share the same Runnable object's state if not properly designed.
